at0105.1.description: Una instrucció addicional dirigida principalment a l'individu/pacient o cuidadors.
at0105.1.comment: Per exemple: "Per a reduir la pressió arterial", "per a diluir la sang". Aquest element de dades permet múltiples ocurrències i s'ha de codificar amb terminologia de referència, sempre que sigui possible. Si és necessari, és possible utilitzar un atribut ENLLAÇ per associar aquest element amb, per exemple, el "diagnòstic original" en una composició de llista de problemes separada, però la indicació s'ha de registrar explícitament aquí, ja que l'ENLLAÇ objectiu pot canviar amb el temps.

at0106.1.description: Una instrucció addicional dirigida principalment a la persona que dispensa l'element ordenat.
at0106.1.comment: Exemple: Instruccions detallades per a la dispensació gradual d'opiacis.

at0161.1.text: Data de venciment de la dispensació
at0161.1.description: La data després de la qual l'element ordenat ja no és vàlid per ser dispensat legalment.
at0161.1.comment: En algunes jurisdiccions, les ordres de dispensació poden tenir una data de venciment després de la qual la prescripció ja no es podria dispensar.

at0165.1.description: Descripció del motiu per al registre d'una dosi efectiva diària total.
at0165.1.comment:
Per exemple: "Control d'hipertensió", "Dosi objectiu 20 mg al dia". La descripció es podria utilitzar per identificar un nom, frase o paràgraf sobre el propòsit de registrar la dosi efectiva diària total, generalment per permetre la valoració cap a una dosi objectiva específica.
L'objectiu o dosi objectiva es pot registrar aquí o més comunament com a part d'un arquetip AVALUACIÓ "Objectiu" enllaçat.

at0143.2.description: Detalls estructurats sobre la medicació en general, incloent-hi la concentració, forma i substàncies constituents.
at0143.2.comment: Utilitzar aquest SLOT on s'ha de declarar explícitament la descripció detallada de producte ordenat. Per exemple: la forma, potencia, qualsevol diluent o barreja d'ingredients.

at0004.1.description: Identificador únic per a l'ordre dels medicaments.
at0004.1.comment: Aquest element de dades permet definir més explícitament múltiples ocurrències en temps d'execució, si cal.

at0047.1.description: Una representació de text, analitzable i calculable, de les instruccions.
at0047.1.comment: Generalment això només s'utilitza quan es passa informació entre sistemes heretats. Per exemple: "10mg bd; 20mg n" utilitzat per la sintaxi de dosificació NHS (en desenvolupament).

at0009.1.description: Descripció narrativa completa sobre com s'ha d'utilitzar l'element ordenat.
at0009.1.comment: Aquesta narrativa normalment hauria d'incloure les dades obtingudes en "quantitat de la dosi", "temps d'administració de la dosi" i qualsevol altra instrucció per utilitzar. On les instruccions de la dosi de medicació són totalment portades per les instruccions estructurades i calculables de la dosi, aquest element ha de portar l'equivalent narratiu, generalment autogenerat. Si no és possible representar la "Instrucció de la dosi" prevista completament en forma calculable, no es recomana la representació parcial, i les instruccions només s'han de registrar en forma narrativa utilitzant aquest element de dades.

at0062.1.description: Detalls sobre la seguretat de la medicació del producte ordenat.

at0060.1.description: El nombre d'administracions de l'article ordenat que s'han completat, com a part de l'ordre complet previst, però abans de l'emissió d'aquesta ordre.
at0060.1.comment: Per exemple: Per registrar que el pacient ha pres les dues primeres càpsules d'antibiòtics en un curs de tres dies, abans de l'alta de l'hospital a una residència d'ancians.

at0044.1.description: Una instrucció addicional sobre com utilitzar o emmagatzemar l'element ordenat.
at0044.1.comment: Per exemple: precaucions com a "prendre amb menjar", "evitar la fruita de raïm", "dissoldre en aigua", "preservar en un lloc fresc i sec". Aquest element de dades permet múltiples ocurrències i s'ha de codificar amb terminologia de referència, sempre que sigui possible.

at0000.1.description: Una ordre per una medicació, vacuna, producte nutricional o altre element terapèutic per a un individu identificat.

at0065.1.description: Detalls sobre la quantitat de l'article ordenat a dispensació.
at0065.1.comment: Aquest SLOT permet gestionar la variació local en diferents jurisdiccions pel que fa a la quantitat de dispensació medicaments.

at0064.1.description: Confirmació per part del prescriptor que la dosi normal o un altre límit de seguretat s'ha anul·lat a causa de circumstàncies excepcionals.

at0000.description: Una prescripción para un medicamento, vacuna, producto nutricional o otro producto terapéutico para un individuo específico

at0002.description: @ internal @

at0004.description: *Unique identifier for the medication order.(en)
at0004.comment: *This data element allows for multiple occurrences to be defined more explicitly at run-time, if required.(en)

at0005.description: @ internal @

at0008.description: *Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.(en)
at0008.comment: *For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.(en)

at0009.description: *Complete narrative description about how the ordered item is to be used.(en)
at0009.comment: *This narrative should normally subsume data captured in 'Dose amount', 'Dose timing' and any additional instructions for use. Where the medication dose directions are fully carried by the structured, computable dose directions, this element should carry the narrative equivalent, generally auto-generated. If it is not possible to represent the intended 'Dose direction' fully in computable form, partial representation is not recommended, and the directions should be only recorded in narrative form using this data element.(en)

at0011.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the commencement of administration or use.(en)
at0011.comment: *For example: 'Start if symptoms recur'. This is intended for a general pre-condition which should trigger the whole medication course to be started, and not for 'as required' administrations of an ongoing order.(en)

at0012.description: *The date and optional time to commence use of the ordered item.(en)

at0013.description: *The date and optional time when it is planned to cease use of the ordered item.(en)

at0016.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the cessation of administration or use.(en)

at0018.description: *The clinical reason for use of the ordered item.(en)
at0018.comment: *For example: 'Angina'. Coding of the clinical indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element allows multiple occurrences. It is not intended to carry an indication for administrative authorisation purposes.(en)

at0044.description: *An additional instruction on how to use or store the ordered item.(en)
at0044.comment: *For example: precautions as 'take with food', 'Avoid grapefruit', 'Dissolve in water', 'store in a cool, dry place'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible.(en)

at0047.description: *A parsable, computable text representation of the directions.(en)
at0047.comment: *Generally this is only used when passing information between legacy systems. For example: '10mg bd; 20mg n' as used by the NHS Dose syntax (in development).(en)

at0050.description: *The time period during which the individual/patient has already been using the ordered item as a part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0050.comment: *For example: To record that the patient had been taking antibiotics 3 days prior to hospital admission, in the context of a 7 day course.(en)

at0051.description: *Details about the maximum, cumulative dose allowed over a defined period for the ordered item.(en)
at0051.comment: *For example: 'up to 1gram per day'. This cluster allows multiple occurrences to enable representation of multiple maximum doses that apply to different time periods. Lifetime maximum dose should not be carried here as it refers to multiple orders over time.(en)

at0053.description: *The period of time during which the maximum dose is calculated.(en)

at0060.description: *The number of administrations of the ordered item that have been completed, as part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0060.comment: *For example: To record that the patient has taken the two first antibiotic tablets of a three day course, prior to discharge from the hospital to a nursing home.(en)

at0062.description: *Details about medication safety for the ordered item.(en)

at0064.description: *Confirmation by the prescriber that the normal dose or other safety limit has been overridden due to exceptional circumstances.(en)

at0065.description: *Details about the amount of the ordered item to be dispensed.(en)
at0065.comment: *This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication dispensing amount to be managed.(en)

at0069.description: *Details of authorisation of the ordered item, including supporting local self-administration, issue and endorsement policies.(en)
at0069.comment: *For example: details of repeat/refill supply. This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication authorisation and re-authorisation to be managed.(en)

at0070.description: *Name of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic/prescribable item being ordered.(en)
at0070.comment: *Depending on the prescribing context this field could be used for either generic- or product-based prescribing. This data field can be used to record tightly bound orders of different medications when they are prescribed as a single pack. It is strongly recommended that the 'Medication item' be coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support, where possible. The extent of coding may vary from the simple name of the medication item through to structured details about the actual medication pack to be used. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available.(en)

at0091.description: *The route by which the ordered item is to be administered into the subject's body.(en)
at0091.comment:
*For example: 'oral', 'intravenous', or 'topical'.
Coding of the route with a terminology is preferred, where possible. Multiple potential routes may be specified.(en)

at0092.description: *Name of the site of administration of the ordered item.(en)
at0092.comment:
*For example: 'left upper arm', 'intravenous catheter right hand'.
 Coding of the body site with a terminology is preferred, where possible.(en)

at0093.description: *Structured description of the site of administration of the ordered item.(en)

at0094.description: *The technique or device by which the ordered item is to be administered.(en)
at0094.comment: *For example: ' via Z-track injection'; 'via nebuliser'. Coding of the method with a terminology is preferred, where possible.(en)

at0095.description: *Details of the medical device used to assist administration of the ordered item.(en)

at0105.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the individual/patient or carers.(en)
at0105.comment: *For example: 'To reduce your blood pressure', 'To thin your blood'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible. If required it is possible to use a LINK attribute to associate this element with, for example, the 'original diagnosis' in a separate Problem list composition but the indication should be explicitly recorded here, as the link target may change over time.(en)

at0106.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the person dispensing the ordered item.(en)
at0106.comment: *Example: Detailed instructions for phased dispensing of opiates.(en)

at0107.description: *An additional instruction which gives advice on recommended or required monitoring of the ordered item.(en)
at0107.comment: *For example: 'Please check renal function in 2 weeks'. This data element allows multiple occurrences.(en)

at0112.description: *Summary information about use of the ordered item, such as current status or key dates, generally used in non-prescription contexts.(en)
at0112.comment: *Course summary may be required when transmitting medication information between systems or representing a FHIR Medication statement. For example: as part of a referral, emergency patient summary or discharge communication.(en)

at0129.description: *Directions about the dispensing of the ordered item.(en)

at0130.description: *The maximum, cumulative amount of medication which should be administered within the allowed period.(en)

at0132.description: *Permission for substitution with a bioequivalent item.(en)
at0132.comment: *In many jurisdictions, substitution of an ordered item as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases substitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.(en)

at0139.description: *An indicator of the urgency with which the ordered item should be dispensed.(en)
at0139.comment: *For example 'Urgent'. This is left to be templated or specialised according to local valuesets. Some jurisdictions allow instructions to dispense to be marked as 'urgent' so that the dispensary handles them quickly.(en)

at0143.description: *Structured details about the overall medication including strength, form and constituent substances.(en)
at0143.comment: *Use this SLOT where the detailed description of the ordered item needs to be explicitly stated. For example: the form, strength, any diluents or mixture of ingredients.(en)

at0148.description: *The overall therapeutic intent of the ordered item.(en)

at0150.description: *Details about the total daily amount of the ordered item expected to be administered.(en)
at0150.comment: *The total daily dose records the expected effective dose, and thus support titration of an initial low dose towards the effective dose over a period of time. This is not itself the target dose or goal but the actual effective daily dose against that goal. In some circumstances the Total daily dose will be calcuable from computable directions, in others it may need to be manually entered.(en)

at0151.description: *The amount of ordered item which will be taken each day if administered as intended.(en)

at0154.description: *The reason why an ordered item should not be substituted at dispense.(en)

at0155.description: *The date from which the ordered item is permitted to be dispensed for the first time.(en)
at0155.comment: *May be used to control the time of dispensing of medications with potential for abuse. For example: benzodiazepines or analgesics.(en)

at0161.description: *The date after which the ordered item is no longer valid to be legally dispensed.(en)
at0161.comment: *In some jurisdictions, orders to dispense may have an expiry date after which the prescription may not be dispensed.(en)

at0162.description: *The reason for a clinical safety or maximum dose override.(en)
at0162.comment: *For example: 'Not responsive at maximum recommended dose. Critical situation.', 'Peniciilin is only valid treatment, likelihood of true penicillin allergy is low'.(en)

at0165.description: *Description of the reason for recording a total daily effective dose.(en)
at0165.comment:
*For example: 'Hypertension control', 'Target dose 20mg daily'. The description could be used to identify a name, phrase or paragraph about the purpose for recording total daily effective dose, commonly to allow to titration towards a specific target dose.
The goal or target dose might be recorded here or more commonly as part of a linked EVALUATION 'Goal' archetype.(en)

at0166.description: *Additional structured details about the ordered item not captured in other fields.(en)

at0167.description: *Additional narrative about the medication order not captured in other fields.(en)

at0168.description: *Substitution of the medication with a bioequivalent medication is allowed.(en)

at0169.description: *Substitution of the medication with a bioequivalent medication is not allowed.(en)

at0170.comment: *This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication dispensing.(en)

at0171.description: *A known or recognised safety issue which applies to this subject and the ordered item, such as an allergy, drug duplication warning or contraindication. This element is normally only recorded when this safety advice is overridden.(en)

at0173.description: *A narrative description of a specific part of overall directions.(en)
at0173.comment: *In some settings, it is common to split overall narrative directions into more specific segments of narrative. For example in hospital settings, it can be common to split the overall directions into 'Dose amount' and 'Dose timing', both as narrative text. These terms can be applied at template or run-time.(en)

at0174.description: *A description of the justification used to calculate a dose amount or administration rate where this is dependent on some other factor.(en)

at0177.description: *Details of structured dose and timing directions.(en)
at0177.comment: *This SLOT has been designed to be used with the CLUSTER.therapeutic_direction archetype to enable recording of a complex set of dosage and timing directions. While it's possible to use other CLUSTER archetypes within this SLOT, it's strongly advised to do so only when predictable queryability is not a priority.(en)

at0178.description: *Overriding of a known or recognised safety issue which applies to this subject and the ordered item, such as an allergy, drug duplication warning or contraindication.(en)

at0179.description: *Advice to future prescribers/dispensers about the ongoing, long term order.(en)
at0179.comment: *For example: 'don't allow brand substitution' or advice regarding using a specific brand to avoid fillers that cause reactions.(en)

at0179.1.description: *Advice to future prescribers/dispensers about the ongoing, long term order.(en)
at0179.1.comment: *For example: 'don't allow brand substitution' or advice regarding using a specific brand to avoid fillers that cause reactions.(en)

at0178.1.description: *Overriding of a known or recognised safety issue which applies to this subject and the ordered item, such as an allergy, drug duplication warning or contraindication.(en)

at0132.1.description: *Permission for substitution with a bioequivalent item.(en)
at0132.1.comment: *In many jurisdictions, substitution of an ordered item as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases substitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.(en)

at0091.1.description: *The route by which the ordered item is to be administered into the subject's body.(en)
at0091.1.comment:
*For example: 'oral', 'intravenous', or 'topical'.
Coding of the route with a terminology is preferred, where possible. Multiple potential routes may be specified.(en)

at0092.1.description: *Name of the site of administration of the ordered item.(en)
at0092.1.comment:
*For example: 'left upper arm', 'intravenous catheter right hand'.
 Coding of the body site with a terminology is preferred, where possible.(en)

at0130.1.description: *The maximum, cumulative amount of medication which should be administered within the allowed period.(en)

at0070.1.description: *Name of the medication, vaccine or other therapeutic/prescribable item being ordered.(en)
at0070.1.comment: *Depending on the prescribing context this field could be used for either generic- or product-based prescribing. This data field can be used to record tightly bound orders of different medications when they are prescribed as a single pack. It is strongly recommended that the 'Medication item' be coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support, where possible. The extent of coding may vary from the simple name of the medication item through to structured details about the actual medication pack to be used. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available.(en)

at0139.1.description: *An indicator of the urgency with which the ordered item should be dispensed.(en)
at0139.1.comment: *For example 'Urgent'. This is left to be templated or specialised according to local valuesets. Some jurisdictions allow instructions to dispense to be marked as 'urgent' so that the dispensary handles them quickly.(en)

at0171.1.description: *A known or recognised safety issue which applies to this subject and the ordered item, such as an allergy, drug duplication warning or contraindication. This element is normally only recorded when this safety advice is overridden.(en)

at0173.1.description: *A narrative description of a specific part of overall directions.(en)
at0173.1.comment: *In some settings, it is common to split overall narrative directions into more specific segments of narrative. For example in hospital settings, it can be common to split the overall directions into 'Dose amount' and 'Dose timing', both as narrative text. These terms can be applied at template or run-time.(en)

at0150.1.description: *Details about the total daily amount of the ordered item expected to be administered.(en)
at0150.1.comment: *The total daily dose records the expected effective dose, and thus support titration of an initial low dose towards the effective dose over a period of time. This is not itself the target dose or goal but the actual effective daily dose against that goal. In some circumstances the Total daily dose will be calcuable from computable directions, in others it may need to be manually entered.(en)

at0151.1.description: *The amount of ordered item which will be taken each day if administered as intended.(en)

at0174.1.description: *A description of the justification used to calculate a dose amount or administration rate where this is dependent on some other factor.(en)

at0154.1.description: *The reason why an ordered item should not be substituted at dispense.(en)

at0155.1.description: *The date from which the ordered item is permitted to be dispensed for the first time.(en)
at0155.1.comment: *May be used to control the time of dispensing of medications with potential for abuse. For example: benzodiazepines or analgesics.(en)

at0013.1.description: *The date and optional time when it is planned to cease use of the ordered item.(en)

at0016.1.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the cessation of administration or use.(en)

at0018.1.description: *The clinical reason for use of the ordered item.(en)
at0018.1.comment: *For example: 'Angina'. Coding of the clinical indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element allows multiple occurrences. It is not intended to carry an indication for administrative authorisation purposes.(en)

at0051.1.description: *Details about the maximum, cumulative dose allowed over a defined period for the ordered item.(en)
at0051.1.comment: *For example: 'up to 1gram per day'. This cluster allows multiple occurrences to enable representation of multiple maximum doses that apply to different time periods. Lifetime maximum dose should not be carried here as it refers to multiple orders over time.(en)

at0050.1.description: *The time period during which the individual/patient has already been using the ordered item as a part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0050.1.comment: *For example: To record that the patient had been taking antibiotics 3 days prior to hospital admission, in the context of a 7 day course.(en)

at0094.1.description: *The technique or device by which the ordered item is to be administered.(en)
at0094.1.comment: *For example: ' via Z-track injection'; 'via nebuliser'. Coding of the method with a terminology is preferred, where possible.(en)

at0053.1.description: *The period of time during which the maximum dose is calculated.(en)

at0011.1.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the commencement of administration or use.(en)
at0011.1.comment: *For example: 'Start if symptoms recur'. This is intended for a general pre-condition which should trigger the whole medication course to be started, and not for 'as required' administrations of an ongoing order.(en)

at0012.1.description: *The date and optional time to commence use of the ordered item.(en)

at0167.1.description: *Additional narrative about the medication order not captured in other fields.(en)

at0148.1.description: *The overall therapeutic intent of the ordered item.(en)

at0107.1.description: *An additional instruction which gives advice on recommended or required monitoring of the ordered item.(en)
at0107.1.comment: *For example: 'Please check renal function in 2 weeks'. This data element allows multiple occurrences.(en)

at0129.1.description: *Directions about the dispensing of the ordered item.(en)

at0162.1.description: *The reason for a clinical safety or maximum dose override.(en)
at0162.1.comment: *For example: 'Not responsive at maximum recommended dose. Critical situation.', 'Peniciilin is only valid treatment, likelihood of true penicillin allergy is low'.(en)

at0105.1.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the individual/patient or carers.(en)
at0105.1.comment: *For example: 'To reduce your blood pressure', 'To thin your blood'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible. If required it is possible to use a LINK attribute to associate this element with, for example, the 'original diagnosis' in a separate Problem list composition but the indication should be explicitly recorded here, as the link target may change over time.(en)

at0106.1.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the person dispensing the ordered item.(en)
at0106.1.comment: *Example: Detailed instructions for phased dispensing of opiates.(en)

at0161.1.description: *The date after which the ordered item is no longer valid to be legally dispensed.(en)
at0161.1.comment: *In some jurisdictions, orders to dispense may have an expiry date after which the prescription may not be dispensed.(en)

at0165.1.description: *Description of the reason for recording a total daily effective dose.(en)
at0165.1.comment:
*For example: 'Hypertension control', 'Target dose 20mg daily'. The description could be used to identify a name, phrase or paragraph about the purpose for recording total daily effective dose, commonly to allow to titration towards a specific target dose.
The goal or target dose might be recorded here or more commonly as part of a linked EVALUATION 'Goal' archetype.(en)

at0143.2.description: *Structured details about the overall medication including strength, form and constituent substances.(en)
at0143.2.comment: *Use this SLOT where the detailed description of the ordered item needs to be explicitly stated. For example: the form, strength, any diluents or mixture of ingredients.(en)

at0004.1.description: *Unique identifier for the medication order.(en)
at0004.1.comment: *This data element allows for multiple occurrences to be defined more explicitly at run-time, if required.(en)

at0047.1.description: *A parsable, computable text representation of the directions.(en)
at0047.1.comment: *Generally this is only used when passing information between legacy systems. For example: '10mg bd; 20mg n' as used by the NHS Dose syntax (in development).(en)

at0009.1.description: *Complete narrative description about how the ordered item is to be used.(en)
at0009.1.comment: *This narrative should normally subsume data captured in 'Dose amount', 'Dose timing' and any additional instructions for use. Where the medication dose directions are fully carried by the structured, computable dose directions, this element should carry the narrative equivalent, generally auto-generated. If it is not possible to represent the intended 'Dose direction' fully in computable form, partial representation is not recommended, and the directions should be only recorded in narrative form using this data element.(en)

at0062.1.description: *Details about medication safety for the ordered item.(en)

at0060.1.description: *The number of administrations of the ordered item that have been completed, as part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0060.1.comment: *For example: To record that the patient has taken the two first antibiotic tablets of a three day course, prior to discharge from the hospital to a nursing home.(en)

at0044.1.description: *An additional instruction on how to use or store the ordered item.(en)
at0044.1.comment: *For example: precautions as 'take with food', 'Avoid grapefruit', 'Dissolve in water', 'store in a cool, dry place'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible.(en)

at0000.1.description: Una prescripción para un medicamento, vacuna, producto nutricional o otro producto terapéutico para un individuo específico

at0065.1.description: *Details about the amount of the ordered item to be dispensed.(en)
at0065.1.comment: *This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication dispensing amount to be managed.(en)

at0064.1.description: *Confirmation by the prescriber that the normal dose or other safety limit has been overridden due to exceptional circumstances.(en)

at0000.description: Preskripcia lieku, vakcíny, dietetickej potraviny alebo iného terapeutického produktu pre identifikovaného pacienta.

at0002.description: @ internal @

at0004.description: *Unique identifier for the medication order.(en)
at0004.comment: *This data element allows for multiple occurrences to be defined more explicitly at run-time, if required.(en)

at0005.description: @ internal @

at0008.description: *Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.(en)
at0008.comment: *For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.(en)

at0009.description: Kompletný popis k podávaniu preskribovanej položky
at0009.comment: Tento popis zvyčajne zahŕňa údaje zadané v štrukturovaných detailoch pre "Dávkovanie" a Časy podania" a tiež iné doplňujúce inštrukcie pre podanie. V prípadoch, kedy je dávkovanie kompletne zadané v štrukturovaných detailoch, tento element by mal obsahovať ich naratívny ekvivalent - automaticky generovaný. Ak nie je možné zapisovať pokyny a dávkovanie v plne štrukturovanom detaile, čiastočne štrukturovaný zápis nie je odporúčaný - v tomto prípade by mali byť zapísané iba v tomto elemente voľným textom.

at0011.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the commencement of administration or use.(en)
at0011.comment: *For example: 'Start if symptoms recur'. This is intended for a general pre-condition which should trigger the whole medication course to be started, and not for 'as required' administrations of an ongoing order.(en)

at0012.description: *The date and optional time to commence use of the ordered item.(en)

at0013.description: *The date and optional time when it is planned to cease use of the ordered item.(en)

at0016.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the cessation of administration or use.(en)

at0018.description: *The clinical reason for use of the ordered item.(en)
at0018.comment: *For example: 'Angina'. Coding of the clinical indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element allows multiple occurrences. It is not intended to carry an indication for administrative authorisation purposes.(en)

at0044.description: *An additional instruction on how to use or store the ordered item.(en)
at0044.comment: *For example: precautions as 'take with food', 'Avoid grapefruit', 'Dissolve in water', 'store in a cool, dry place'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible.(en)

at0047.description: Pokyny zadané vo forme parsovateľného , vypočítateľného textu
at0047.comment: Vo všeobecnosti sa použije v prípade migrácie dát z legacy (predchádzajúcich) systémov.

at0050.description: *The time period during which the individual/patient has already been using the ordered item as a part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0050.comment: *For example: To record that the patient had been taking antibiotics 3 days prior to hospital admission, in the context of a 7 day course.(en)

at0051.description: Detaily ohľadom maximálnej kumulatívnej dávky počas definovaného časového intervalu
at0051.comment: Napríklad: "najviac1g/deň". Tento cluster môže mať viacpočetný výskyt, aby bolo možné zadať viacej reprezentácií maximálnej dávky pre rôzne časové periódy. Na tomto mieste by ale nemala byť evidovaná maximálna celoživotná dávka, kedže tá môže odkazovať na viacero preskripcií

at0053.description: Časový interval pre výpočet maximálnej dávky

at0060.description: *The number of administrations of the ordered item that have been completed, as part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0060.comment: *For example: To record that the patient has taken the two first antibiotic tablets of a three day course, prior to discharge from the hospital to a nursing home.(en)

at0062.description: Detaily k bezpečnosti medikácie pre preskribovanú položku

at0064.description: Potvrdenie , že preskribujúci predpísal vyššiu dávku ako je norma, prípadne , že boli prekročené iné bezpečnostné limity kvôli výnimočným okolnostiam

at0065.description: *Details about the amount of the ordered item to be dispensed.(en)
at0065.comment: *This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication dispensing amount to be managed.(en)

at0069.description: *Details of authorisation of the ordered item, including supporting local self-administration, issue and endorsement policies.(en)
at0069.comment: *For example: details of repeat/refill supply. This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication authorisation and re-authorisation to be managed.(en)

at0070.description: Názov lieku /liečiva, vakcíny alebo inej preskribovanej položky
at0070.comment: V závislosti od kontextu by toto pole malo byť použité pre generickú preskripciu alebo pre preskripciu konkrétneho lieku. Takisto aj na predpis rôznych liekov/liečív, ktoré sú predpisované spolu ako jeden celok (zložený liek pre infúziu alebo magistraliter). Ak je to možné, dôrazne sa odporúča kódovanie terminológiou , kvôli následnému spúšťaniu procesov pre podporu rozhodovania. Rozsah kódovania môže byť rôzny - od jednoduchého názvu lieku, až po štrukturované detaily danej zmesi. Voľný text by mal byť použitý , iba v prípade, že žiadna vhodná terminológia nie je dostupná.

at0091.description: Cesta podania predpísaná pre danú preskribovanú položku
at0091.comment: Napríklad: per.os. , I.V., Odporúča sa kódovanie terminológiou, ak je to možné. V prípade potreby môže byť špecifikovaných viacero ciest podania

at0092.description: Názov časti tela, kde má byť podaná preskribovaná položka
at0092.comment: Napríklad: "ľavá horná končatina". i.v.katéter pravá ruka". Odporúča sa kódovanie terminológiou, ak je to možné

at0093.description: Štrukturovaný popis časti tela , do ktorej má byť podaná preskribovaná položka

at0094.description: Technika alebo zariadenie, ktorým má byť podaná preskribovaná položka
at0094.comment: Napríklad: !cez Z-track inj. ", "cez nebulizér". Odporúča sa kódovanie terminológiou, ak je to možné

at0095.description: Detaily zariadenia, ktoré má byť použité na podanie preskribovanej položky.

at0105.comment: *For example: 'To reduce your blood pressure', 'To thin your blood'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible. If required it is possible to use a LINK attribute to associate this element with, for example, the 'original diagnosis' in a separate Problem list composition but the indication should be explicitly recorded here, as the link target may change over time.(en)

at0106.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the person dispensing the ordered item.(en)
at0106.comment: *Example: Detailed instructions for phased dispensing of opiates.(en)

at0107.description: *An additional instruction which gives advice on recommended or required monitoring of the ordered item.(en)
at0107.comment: *For example: 'Please check renal function in 2 weeks'. This data element allows multiple occurrences.(en)

at0112.description: *Summary information about use of the ordered item, such as current status or key dates, generally used in non-prescription contexts.(en)
at0112.comment: *Course summary may be required when transmitting medication information between systems or representing a FHIR Medication statement. For example: as part of a referral, emergency patient summary or discharge communication.(en)

at0129.description: *Directions about the dispensing of the ordered item.(en)

at0130.description: Maximálna kumulatívna dávka počas definovaného časového intervalu

at0132.comment: *In many jurisdictions, substitution of an ordered item as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases substitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.(en)

at0139.description: *An indicator of the urgency with which the ordered item should be dispensed.(en)
at0139.comment: *For example 'Urgent'. This is left to be templated or specialised according to local valuesets. Some jurisdictions allow instructions to dispense to be marked as 'urgent' so that the dispensary handles them quickly.(en)

at0143.description: Štrukturované detaily predpísanej položky, vrátane sily, formy a jednotlivé ingrediencie v zmesi
at0143.comment: Použite tento slot v prípade, že je potrebný detailný popis preskribovanej položky. Napríklad: sila, forma, roztoky alebo zmesi jednotlivých ingrediencií

at0148.description: *The overall therapeutic intent of the ordered item.(en)

at0150.description: Detaily ohľadom celkovej dennej dávky preskribovanej položky
at0150.comment: Celková denná dávka obsahuje očakávanú efektívnu dávku, vďaka čomu podporuje titráciu úvodnej nízkej dávky oproti efektívnej dávke za časový interval. Tzn, nie je to konečná dávka alebo cieľ ale aktuálna efektívna dávka oproti tomuto cieľu. V niektorých situáciách bude celková denná dávka vypočítateľná zo štruktorvaného dávkovania, v iných tju bude nutné zadať manuálne.

at0151.description: *The amount of ordered item which will be taken each day if administered as intended.(en)

at0154.description: *The reason why an ordered item should not be substituted at dispense.(en)

at0155.description: *The date from which the ordered item is permitted to be dispensed for the first time.(en)
at0155.comment: *May be used to control the time of dispensing of medications with potential for abuse. For example: benzodiazepines or analgesics.(en)

at0161.description: *The date after which the ordered item is no longer valid to be legally dispensed.(en)
at0161.comment: *In some jurisdictions, orders to dispense may have an expiry date after which the prescription may not be dispensed.(en)

at0162.description: Dôvod pre prekročenie bezpečnej dávky alebo klinickej bezpečnosti
at0162.comment: Napríklad: "nereaguje na maximálnu odporúčanú dávku, kritická situácia ", "Penicilín je jediná vhodná liečba, pravdepodobnosť alergie je nízka"

at0165.description: Popis k dôvodu pre záznam k celkovej dennej efektívnej dávke

at0166.description: *Additional structured details about the ordered item not captured in other fields.(en)

at0167.description: *Additional narrative about the medication order not captured in other fields.(en)

at0168.description: *Substitution of the medication with a bioequivalent medication is allowed.(en)

at0169.description: *Substitution of the medication with a bioequivalent medication is not allowed.(en)

at0170.comment: *This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication dispensing.(en)

at0171.description: Prekročenie známej alebo rozpoznanej bezpečnosti pri preskribovaní položky danému pacientovi , ako napr. alergia, opakované podanie alebo kontraindikácia. Tento element bude vyplnený iba v prípade, že je prekročená bezpečnosť

at0173.description: Popis špecifickej časti pokynov voľným textom
at0173.comment: V niektorých prípadoch je bežné rozdeliť celkové pokyny k podaniu na niekoľko špecifických častí. Napr. počas hospitalizácie je možné to rozdeliť na časti "Množstvo dávky" a "Časy podania" - obe ako voľný text. To potom môže byť použité v šablón alebo v run-time

at0174.description: Popis oddôvodnenia pre výočet veľkosti dávky alebo rýchlosti podania , v prípade, že to závisí od nejakých iných faktorov

at0177.description: Detaily pre štrukturované dávkovanie a časy podania
at0177.comment: Tento slot slúži na použite s archetypom CLUSTER.therapeutic_direction - pre umožnenie zápisu komplexného dávkovania a časov podania. Napriek tomu, že je možné do tohoto slotu vkladať aj iný cluster, dôsledne odporúčame robiť tak iba v prípade , že predpovedateľné dotazovanie nie je prioritou

at0178.description: Prekročenie známej alebo rozpoznanej bezpečnosti pri preskribovaní položky danému pacientovi , ako napr. alergia, opakované podanie alebo kontraindikácia

at0179.comment: *For example: 'don't allow brand substitution' or advice regarding using a specific brand to avoid fillers that cause reactions.(en)

at0179.1.description: *Advice to future prescribers/dispensers about the ongoing, long term order.(en)
at0179.1.comment: *For example: 'don't allow brand substitution' or advice regarding using a specific brand to avoid fillers that cause reactions.(en)

at0178.1.description: Prekročenie známej alebo rozpoznanej bezpečnosti pri preskribovaní položky danému pacientovi , ako napr. alergia, opakované podanie alebo kontraindikácia

at0132.1.description: *Permission for substitution with a bioequivalent item.(en)
at0132.1.comment: *In many jurisdictions, substitution of an ordered item as a generic form or with a different brand name, which has been determined as bioequivalent, is allowed at the point of dispense or supply. In other cases substitution is assumed and the clinician has to explicitly request non-substitution.(en)

at0091.1.description: Cesta podania predpísaná pre danú preskribovanú položku
at0091.1.comment: Napríklad: per.os. , I.V., Odporúča sa kódovanie terminológiou, ak je to možné. V prípade potreby môže byť špecifikovaných viacero ciest podania

at0092.1.description: Názov časti tela, kde má byť podaná preskribovaná položka
at0092.1.comment: Napríklad: "ľavá horná končatina". i.v.katéter pravá ruka". Odporúča sa kódovanie terminológiou, ak je to možné

at0130.1.description: Maximálna kumulatívna dávka počas definovaného časového intervalu

at0070.1.description: Názov lieku /liečiva, vakcíny alebo inej preskribovanej položky
at0070.1.comment: V závislosti od kontextu by toto pole malo byť použité pre generickú preskripciu alebo pre preskripciu konkrétneho lieku. Takisto aj na predpis rôznych liekov/liečív, ktoré sú predpisované spolu ako jeden celok (zložený liek pre infúziu alebo magistraliter). Ak je to možné, dôrazne sa odporúča kódovanie terminológiou , kvôli následnému spúšťaniu procesov pre podporu rozhodovania. Rozsah kódovania môže byť rôzny - od jednoduchého názvu lieku, až po štrukturované detaily danej zmesi. Voľný text by mal byť použitý , iba v prípade, že žiadna vhodná terminológia nie je dostupná.

at0139.1.description: *An indicator of the urgency with which the ordered item should be dispensed.(en)
at0139.1.comment: *For example 'Urgent'. This is left to be templated or specialised according to local valuesets. Some jurisdictions allow instructions to dispense to be marked as 'urgent' so that the dispensary handles them quickly.(en)

at0171.1.description: Prekročenie známej alebo rozpoznanej bezpečnosti pri preskribovaní položky danému pacientovi , ako napr. alergia, opakované podanie alebo kontraindikácia. Tento element bude vyplnený iba v prípade, že je prekročená bezpečnosť

at0173.1.description: Popis špecifickej časti pokynov voľným textom
at0173.1.comment: V niektorých prípadoch je bežné rozdeliť celkové pokyny k podaniu na niekoľko špecifických častí. Napr. počas hospitalizácie je možné to rozdeliť na časti "Množstvo dávky" a "Časy podania" - obe ako voľný text. To potom môže byť použité v šablón alebo v run-time

at0150.1.description: Detaily ohľadom celkovej dennej dávky preskribovanej položky
at0150.1.comment: Celková denná dávka obsahuje očakávanú efektívnu dávku, vďaka čomu podporuje titráciu úvodnej nízkej dávky oproti efektívnej dávke za časový interval. Tzn, nie je to konečná dávka alebo cieľ ale aktuálna efektívna dávka oproti tomuto cieľu. V niektorých situáciách bude celková denná dávka vypočítateľná zo štruktorvaného dávkovania, v iných tju bude nutné zadať manuálne.

at0151.1.description: *The amount of ordered item which will be taken each day if administered as intended.(en)

at0174.1.description: Popis oddôvodnenia pre výočet veľkosti dávky alebo rýchlosti podania , v prípade, že to závisí od nejakých iných faktorov

at0154.1.description: *The reason why an ordered item should not be substituted at dispense.(en)

at0155.1.description: *The date from which the ordered item is permitted to be dispensed for the first time.(en)
at0155.1.comment: *May be used to control the time of dispensing of medications with potential for abuse. For example: benzodiazepines or analgesics.(en)

at0013.1.description: *The date and optional time when it is planned to cease use of the ordered item.(en)

at0016.1.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the cessation of administration or use.(en)

at0018.1.description: *The clinical reason for use of the ordered item.(en)
at0018.1.comment: *For example: 'Angina'. Coding of the clinical indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element allows multiple occurrences. It is not intended to carry an indication for administrative authorisation purposes.(en)

at0051.1.description: Detaily ohľadom maximálnej kumulatívnej dávky počas definovaného časového intervalu
at0051.1.comment: Napríklad: "najviac1g/deň". Tento cluster môže mať viacpočetný výskyt, aby bolo možné zadať viacej reprezentácií maximálnej dávky pre rôzne časové periódy. Na tomto mieste by ale nemala byť evidovaná maximálna celoživotná dávka, kedže tá môže odkazovať na viacero preskripcií

at0050.1.description: *The time period during which the individual/patient has already been using the ordered item as a part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0050.1.comment: *For example: To record that the patient had been taking antibiotics 3 days prior to hospital admission, in the context of a 7 day course.(en)

at0094.1.description: Technika alebo zariadenie, ktorým má byť podaná preskribovaná položka
at0094.1.comment: Napríklad: !cez Z-track inj. ", "cez nebulizér". Odporúča sa kódovanie terminológiou, ak je to možné

at0053.1.description: Časový interval pre výpočet maximálnej dávky

at0011.1.description: *A condition which, when met, requires the commencement of administration or use.(en)
at0011.1.comment: *For example: 'Start if symptoms recur'. This is intended for a general pre-condition which should trigger the whole medication course to be started, and not for 'as required' administrations of an ongoing order.(en)

at0012.1.description: *The date and optional time to commence use of the ordered item.(en)

at0167.1.description: *Additional narrative about the medication order not captured in other fields.(en)

at0148.1.description: *The overall therapeutic intent of the ordered item.(en)

at0107.1.description: *An additional instruction which gives advice on recommended or required monitoring of the ordered item.(en)
at0107.1.comment: *For example: 'Please check renal function in 2 weeks'. This data element allows multiple occurrences.(en)

at0129.1.description: *Directions about the dispensing of the ordered item.(en)

at0162.1.description: Dôvod pre prekročenie bezpečnej dávky alebo klinickej bezpečnosti
at0162.1.comment: Napríklad: "nereaguje na maximálnu odporúčanú dávku, kritická situácia ", "Penicilín je jediná vhodná liečba, pravdepodobnosť alergie je nízka"

at0105.1.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the individual/patient or carers.(en)
at0105.1.comment: *For example: 'To reduce your blood pressure', 'To thin your blood'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible. If required it is possible to use a LINK attribute to associate this element with, for example, the 'original diagnosis' in a separate Problem list composition but the indication should be explicitly recorded here, as the link target may change over time.(en)

at0106.1.description: *An additional instruction directed primarily at the person dispensing the ordered item.(en)
at0106.1.comment: *Example: Detailed instructions for phased dispensing of opiates.(en)

at0161.1.description: *The date after which the ordered item is no longer valid to be legally dispensed.(en)
at0161.1.comment: *In some jurisdictions, orders to dispense may have an expiry date after which the prescription may not be dispensed.(en)

at0165.1.description: Popis k dôvodu pre záznam k celkovej dennej efektívnej dávke

at0143.2.description: Štrukturované detaily predpísanej položky, vrátane sily, formy a jednotlivé ingrediencie v zmesi
at0143.2.comment: Použite tento slot v prípade, že je potrebný detailný popis preskribovanej položky. Napríklad: sila, forma, roztoky alebo zmesi jednotlivých ingrediencií

at0004.1.description: *Unique identifier for the medication order.(en)
at0004.1.comment: *This data element allows for multiple occurrences to be defined more explicitly at run-time, if required.(en)

at0047.1.description: Pokyny zadané vo forme parsovateľného , vypočítateľného textu
at0047.1.comment: Vo všeobecnosti sa použije v prípade migrácie dát z legacy (predchádzajúcich) systémov.

at0009.1.description: Kompletný popis k podávaniu preskribovanej položky
at0009.1.comment: Tento popis zvyčajne zahŕňa údaje zadané v štrukturovaných detailoch pre "Dávkovanie" a Časy podania" a tiež iné doplňujúce inštrukcie pre podanie. V prípadoch, kedy je dávkovanie kompletne zadané v štrukturovaných detailoch, tento element by mal obsahovať ich naratívny ekvivalent - automaticky generovaný. Ak nie je možné zapisovať pokyny a dávkovanie v plne štrukturovanom detaile, čiastočne štrukturovaný zápis nie je odporúčaný - v tomto prípade by mali byť zapísané iba v tomto elemente voľným textom.

at0062.1.description: Detaily k bezpečnosti medikácie pre preskribovanú položku

at0060.1.description: *The number of administrations of the ordered item that have been completed, as part of the intended whole order but prior to the issuance of this order.(en)
at0060.1.comment: *For example: To record that the patient has taken the two first antibiotic tablets of a three day course, prior to discharge from the hospital to a nursing home.(en)

at0044.1.description: *An additional instruction on how to use or store the ordered item.(en)
at0044.1.comment: *For example: precautions as 'take with food', 'Avoid grapefruit', 'Dissolve in water', 'store in a cool, dry place'. This data element allows multiple occurrences and should be coded with a reference terminology, where possible.(en)

at0000.1.description: Preskripcia lieku, vakcíny, dietetickej potraviny alebo iného terapeutického produktu pre identifikovaného pacienta.

at0065.1.description: *Details about the amount of the ordered item to be dispensed.(en)
at0065.1.comment: *This SLOT allows for local variation in the different jurisdictions regarding medication dispensing amount to be managed.(en)

at0064.1.description: Potvrdenie , že preskribujúci predpísal vyššiu dávku ako je norma, prípadne , že boli prekročené iné bezpečnostné limity kvôli výnimočným okolnostiam

author.name: John Tore Valand, Silje Ljosland Bakke

de.purpose: Zur Aufzeichnung einer Aussage über die aktuelle Einnahme eines einzelnen Medikaments durch eine Person zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt.
de.use:
Zur Aufzeichnung einer Aussage über die aktuelle Einnahme eines einzelnen Medikaments durch eine Person zu einem bestimmten Zeitpunkt.

In diesem Zusammenhang beschreibt der Begriff "Medikamente" eine breite Palette von Gegenständen, die verschrieben oder "rezeptfrei" erworben werden können. Dazu gehören:
- einen einzelnen pharmazeutischen Artikel oder Wirkstoff; 
- eine Magistralrezeptur; 
- ein Kombinationspräparat; 
- ein Nahrungsergänzungsmittel; oder 
- ein anderer therapeutischer Artikel, der zur Behandlung oder Vorbeugung von Krankheiten verwendet wird, wie z. B. ein Verband oder eine Wundauflage, die ein antimikrobielles Mittel enthält.

Es wird davon ausgegangen, dass dieser Archetyp üblicherweise in einem Austauschkontext verwendet wird, z. B. als Teil eines Gesundheitsberichts oder eines Berichts über den Übergang der Versorgung, wobei eine oder mehrere Instanzen dieser Datengruppe verwendet werden können, um eine "Aktuelle Medikamentenliste" darzustellen. Zum Beispiel: 
- bei der Aufnahme ins Krankenhaus;
- als Teil einer fachärztlichen Überweisung; oder
- als Grundlage für eine Überprüfung der Medikation.

Die Informationsquelle kann eine Person, ihr Betreuer oder ein Kliniker sein.

Dieser Archetyp wurde so konzipiert, dass er möglichst mit INSTRUCTION.medictation oder ACTION.medication_management übereinstimmt. Er wurde jedoch so eingeschränkt, dass er nur wesentliche Informationen enthält, die für den Austausch oder die Zusammenfassung erforderlich sind, sowie zusätzliche ereignisbasierte Datenelemente wie das Datenelement "Zuletzt verabreicht", um einen nahtlosen Übergang der Versorgung zu unterstützen. 

Für jedes Medikament oder jede Kombinationspackung ist eine Instanz dieses Archetyps zu erfassen. Wenn dasselbe Medikament in verschiedenen Dosierungen oder mit unterschiedlicher Häufigkeit verwendet wird, sollte jede einzelne Dosierungs- und Häufigkeitsänderung als separate Instanz erfasst werden.

Dieser Archetyp sollte nur zum Zeitpunkt der Erstellung als aktuell betrachtet werden. 

Dieser Archetyp wurde so konzipiert, dass er mit der FHIR-Ressource MedicationStatement übereinstimmt, ist aber absichtlich auf die "aktuelle Verwendung" und nicht auf die vergangene oder zukünftige Verwendung beschränkt.
de.misuse:
Nicht für die Aufzeichnung von zusammenfassenden oder dauerhaften Informationen über die frühere Einnahme eines Medikaments zu verwenden - verwenden Sie zu diesem Zweck EVALUATION.medication_summary.

Nicht zur Aufzeichnung von Details über eine Medikamentenverfügung verwenden - verwenden Sie zu diesem Zweck INSTRUCTION.medication_order.

Nicht zur Aufzeichnung von Details über bestimmte medikamentenbezogene Aktivitäten, wie Verabreichung oder Abgabe, zu verwenden - verwenden Sie zu diesem Zweck ACTION.medication.

Nicht zur Erstellung eines Frameworks zum Aufzeichnen von Antworten auf vordefinierte Screening-Fragen zur Verwendung eines bestimmten Medikaments oder einer Medikamentengruppe geeignet. Verwenden Sie zu diesem Zweck OBSERVATION.medication_screening.

Nicht für die Darstellung einer verabreichten Impfung zu verwenden - verwenden Sie zu diesem Zweck einen geeigneten Archetyp.

Nicht zu verwenden, um Informationen über verwendete oder implantierte medizinische Geräte zu erfassen.

nb.purpose: For å registrere en beskrivelse av den nåværende bruken av et enkelt legemiddel for et individ på et spesifisert tidspunkt.
nb.use:
Brukes for å registrere en beskrivelse av den nåværende bruken av et enkelt legemiddel for et individ på et spesifisert tidspunkt.

I denne av denne arketypen brukes en bred definisjon av "legemiddel", og omhandler både legemidler på og uten resept. Dette inkluderer:
- et enkelt farmasøytisk produkt eller middel;
- en tilberedning;
- et kombinasjonsprodukt;
- et ernæringsprodukt; eller
- et annet terapeutisk produkt brukt til å behandle eller forebygge sykdom, for eksempel en bandasje med et antimikrobielt middel.

Denne arketypen vil vanligvis bli brukt ved utveksling av informasjon, for eksempel som en del av en helseoppsummering eller en oppsummering ved overflytting mellom helseinstitusjoner. I slike tilfeller kan en eller flere instanser av denne arketypen brukes til å representere en "Nåværende medikamentliste". For eksempel:
- ved innleggelse på sykehus,
- som en del av en spesialisthenvisning,
- som grunnlag for en medikamentgjennomgang.

Kilden til informasjon kan være en person, deres omsorgsperson eller en kliniker.

Denne arketypen er utformet for å kunne samsvare med INSTRUCTION.medication_order (Legemiddelordinering) eller ACTION.medication (Legemiddelhåndtering) ved behov. Imidlertid er arketypen begrenset til å kun representere essensiell informasjon som er nødvendig for utveksling eller oppsummeringsformål, i tillegg til hendelsesbaserte dataelementer som "Sist administrert". Dette er gjort for å støtte en sømløs omsorgsovergang.

Registrer én forekomst av denne arketypen per legemiddel eller kombinasjonspakke. Brukes samme legemiddel i ulike doser eller dosefrekvenser, skal hver unike doserings- og frekvensvariasjon registreres som en separat forekomst.

Denne arketypen bør bare betraktes som oppdatert på registreringstidspunktet.

Denne arketypen er utformet for å samsvare med FHIR MedicationStatement-ressursen, men er bevisst begrenset til "nåværende bruk", og ikke for tidligere eller fremtidig bruk av et legemiddel.
nb.misuse:
Brukes ikke for å registrere et sammendrag om all bruk av et spesifisert legemiddel over et individs livstid, bruk arketypen EVALUATION.medication_summary (Legemiddelsammendrag) til dette formålet.

Brukes ikke for å dokumentere forordningen av et legemiddel - bruk arketypen INSTRUCTION.medication_order (Legemiddelordinering) til dette formålet.

Brukes ikke for å dokumentere administreringen av et legemiddel - bruk arketypen ACTION.medication (Legemiddelhåndtering) til dette formålet.

Brukes ikke for å lage et rammeverk for registrering av svar på forhåndsdefinerte spørsmål om bruken av et hvilket som helst legemiddel eller gruppe av legemidler. Bruk OBSERVATION.medication_screening (Kartleggingsspørsmål om legemidler) til dette formålet.

Brukes ikke for å dokumentere en vaksine som er administrert - bruk en egnet arketype til dette formålet.

Brukes ikke for å registrere infomrasjon om medisinsk utstyr som er brukt eller implantert. Bruk en egnet arketype til dette formålet.

en.purpose: To record an assertion about the current use of a single medication by an individual at a specified point in time.
en.use:
Use to record an assertion about the current use of a single medication by an individual at a specified point in time.

In this medication statement context, ‘medication’ describes a wide range of items that may be prescribed or obtained 'over the counter'. This includes:
- a single pharmaceutical item or agent; 
- an extemporaneous preparation; 
- a combination therapy product; 
- a nutritional product; or 
- another therapeutic item used to treat or prevent disease, such as a bandage or dressing containing an antimicrobial agent.

It is anticipated that this archetype will commonly be used within an exchange context, for example as part of a health summary or transition of care summary, where one or more instances of this data group may be used to represent a ‘Current medication list’. For example: 
- on admission to hospital;
- as part of a specialist referral; or
- as the basis for a medication review.

The source of information may be an individual, their carer or a clinician.

This archetype has been designed to align with INSTRUCTION.medictation or ACTION.medication_management, where possible. However, it has been constrained to represent only essential information necessary for exchange or summary purposes, plus the addition of event-based data elements such as the ‘Last administered’ data element to support a seamless transition of care. 

Record one instance of this archetype per medication or combination pack. If the same medication is being used in different dose amounts or varying dose frequencies, each unique dosage and frequency variation should be recorded as a separate instance.

This archetype should only be considered up-to-date at the time of authoring. 

This archetype has been designed to align with the FHIR MedicationStatement resource but is intentionally constrained to 'current use', rather than past or future use.
en.misuse:
Not to be used to record summary or persistent information about past use of a medication - use EVALUATION.medication_summary for this purpose.

Not to be used to record details about a medication order - use INSTRUCTION.medication_order for this purpose.

Not to be used to record details about specific medication related activities, such as administration or dispensing - use ACTION.medication for this purpose.

Not to be used to create a framework for recording answers to pre-defined screening questions about the use of any specified medication or grouping of medications - use OBSERVATION.medication_screening for this purpose.

Not to be used to represent a vaccination that has been administered - use an appropriate archetype for this purpose.

Not to be used to record information about medical devices that are used or implanted.

at0000.description: An assertion about the current use of a single medication by an individual.

at0002.description: Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.

at0003.description: @ internal @

at0004.description: @ internal @

at0005.description: Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models or formalisms.
at0005.comment: For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.

at0006.comment: It is strongly recommended that the 'Medication name' be coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support, where possible. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available or for customised extemporaneous preparations. The extent of coding may vary from the simple generic or product name of the medication item through to structured details about the actual medication pack to be used. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available. For example: 'Plaquenil'; 'Ibrutinib 420 mg tablet'; 'Rectinol ointment, 50 g, tube'; or 'Hydrofibre dressing with silver'.

at0023.description: The clinical symptom, sign or diagnosis that necessitates the use of the medication.
at0023.comment: For example: 'Angina' or 'Migraine'. Coding of the clinical indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element has multiple occurrences to allow recording of more than one clinical indication per medication.

at0026.description: The date and time when the medication was last taken by, or administered to, the individual.
at0026.comment: For example: the time warfarin was last taken at home prior to admission to hospital; or when the last dose was administered before a transfer from hospital to aged care.

at0029.description: Additional narrative about the medication statement not captured in other fields.

at0030.description: The route by which the medication is administrated into the body.
at0030.comment: For example: 'oral', 'intravenous', or 'topical'. Coding of the route with a terminology is preferred, where possible.

at0032.description: Narrative description of the use of the medication.

at0037.description: The intended absolute end date for the use of the medication or a textual indication that the medication will be used indefinitely.
at0037.comment: The DV_DATE_TIME datatype can indicate a precise, absolute date and optional time for the intended end of a limited course of medication - for example, the endpoint of a course of antibiotics the sender has just initiated. The DV_CODED_TEXT option can also be used to record that the medication is intended for indefinite use, for example, potentially lifelong use of an antihypertensive medication.

at0038.description: There is no proposed end date for this medication.

at0045.description: Details of structured dose and timing directions.

at0046.description: Structured details about the overall medication including strength, form and constituent substances.

at0047.description: Complete narrative description about how the ordered item is to be used.

at0048.description: Structured details about the medication use.

at0002.1.description: Default, unspecified point in time or interval event which may be explicitly defined in a template or at run-time.

at0045.2.description: Details of structured dose and timing directions.

at0047.1.description: Complete narrative description about how the ordered item is to be used.

at0026.1.description: The date and time when the medication was last taken by, or administered to, the individual.
at0026.1.comment: For example: the time warfarin was last taken at home prior to admission to hospital; or when the last dose was administered before a transfer from hospital to aged care.

at0037.1.description: The intended absolute end date for the use of the medication or a textual indication that the medication will be used indefinitely.
at0037.1.comment: The DV_DATE_TIME datatype can indicate a precise, absolute date and optional time for the intended end of a limited course of medication - for example, the endpoint of a course of antibiotics the sender has just initiated. The DV_CODED_TEXT option can also be used to record that the medication is intended for indefinite use, for example, potentially lifelong use of an antihypertensive medication.

at0046.2.description: Structured details about the overall medication including strength, form and constituent substances.

at0006.1.comment: It is strongly recommended that the 'Medication name' be coded with a terminology capable of triggering decision support, where possible. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available or for customised extemporaneous preparations. The extent of coding may vary from the simple generic or product name of the medication item through to structured details about the actual medication pack to be used. Free text entry should only be used if there is no appropriate terminology available. For example: 'Plaquenil'; 'Ibrutinib 420 mg tablet'; 'Rectinol ointment, 50 g, tube'; or 'Hydrofibre dressing with silver'.

at0029.1.description: Additional narrative about the medication statement not captured in other fields.

at0030.1.description: The route by which the medication is administrated into the body.
at0030.1.comment: For example: 'oral', 'intravenous', or 'topical'. Coding of the route with a terminology is preferred, where possible.

at0032.1.description: Narrative description of the use of the medication.

at0000.1.description: An assertion about the current use of a single medication by an individual.

at0023.1.description: The clinical symptom, sign or diagnosis that necessitates the use of the medication.
at0023.1.comment: For example: 'Angina' or 'Migraine'. Coding of the clinical indication with a terminology is preferred, where possible. This data element has multiple occurrences to allow recording of more than one clinical indication per medication.

at0000.description: Eine Aussage über die aktuelle Einnahme eines einzelnen Medikaments durch eine Person.

at0005.description: Zusätzliche Informationen zur Erfassung lokaler Inhalte oder Anpassung an andere Referenzmodelle/Formalismen.
at0005.comment: Zum Beispiel: Lokaler Informationsbedarf oder zusätzliche Metadaten zur Anpassung an FHIR-Ressourcen.

at0006.description: Name des Medikaments, des Impfstoffs oder eines anderen therapeutischen / verschreibungsfähigen Mittels.
at0006.comment: Es wird dringend empfohlen, den "Medikamentennamen" mit einer Terminologie zu kodieren, die nach Möglichkeit eine Entscheidungshilfe auslösen kann. Freitexteingaben sollten nur dann verwendet werden, wenn keine geeignete Terminologie zur Verfügung steht oder wenn es sich um maßgeschneiderte, improvisierte Zubereitungen handelt. Der Umfang der Kodierung kann vom einfachen generischen oder Produktnamen des Arzneimittels bis hin zu strukturierten Angaben über die tatsächlich zu verwendende Arzneimittelpackung reichen. Freitexteingaben sollten nur verwendet werden, wenn keine geeignete Terminologie verfügbar ist. Zum Beispiel: "Plaquenil"; "Ibrutinib 420 mg Tablette"; "Rectinol-Salbe, 50 g, Tube"; oder "Hydrofaserverband mit Silber".

at0023.description: Der klinische Grund für die Anwendung des Medikaments.
at0023.comment: Zum Beispiel: "Angina" oder "Migräne". Die Kodierung der klinischen Indikation mit einer Terminologie wird, wenn möglich, bevorzugt. Dieses Datenelement ist mehrfach vorhanden, um die Erfassung von mehr als einer klinischen Indikation pro Medikament zu ermöglichen.

at0026.description: Datum und Uhrzeit der letzten Einnahme oder Verabreichung des Medikaments durch die betreffende Person.
at0026.comment: Zum Beispiel: der Zeitpunkt der letzten Einnahme von Warfarin zu Hause vor der Einlieferung ins Krankenhaus; oder wann die letzte Dosis vor einer Verlegung vom Krankenhaus in die Altenpflege verabreicht wurde.

at0029.description: Zusätzliche Kommentare über den Medikationseintrag, die nicht in anderen Feldern erfasst wurden.

at0030.description: Der Weg, über den das Medikament in den Körper gelangt.
at0030.comment: Zum Beispiel: "oral", "intravenös" oder "topisch". Die Kodierung des Verabreichungsweges mit einer Terminologie ist vorzuziehen, sofern dies möglich ist.

at0032.description: Beschreibung über die Anwendung des identifizierten Medikaments.

at0037.description: Das vorgesehene absolute Enddatum für die Verwendung des Medikaments oder ein textlicher Hinweis, dass das Medikament auf unbestimmte Zeit verwendet werden soll.
at0037.comment: Mit dem Datentyp DV_DATE_TIME kann ein genaues, absolutes Datum und optional eine Uhrzeit für das beabsichtigte Ende einer begrenzten Medikamenteneinnahme angegeben werden, z. B. der Endzeitpunkt einer Antibiotikaeinnahme, die der Absender gerade begonnen hat. Die Option DV_CODED_TEXT kann auch verwendet werden, um zu vermerken, dass das Medikament für eine unbefristete Einnahme vorgesehen ist, z. B. für die potenziell lebenslange Einnahme eines blutdrucksenkenden Medikaments.

at0038.description: Es gibt kein vorgeschlagenes Enddatum für dieses Medikament.

at0005.description: Ytterligere informasjon som er nødvendig for å sammenstille med andre referansemodeller/formalismer.
at0005.comment: F.eks. lokale informasjonskrav eller ekstra metadata for å samsvare med FHIR.

at0006.comment:
Det anbefales sterkt at "Legemiddel" kodes med en terminologi som understøtter beslutningsstøtte der dette er mulig, f.eks. FEST. Fritekst bør bare brukes om ingen passende terminologi er tilgjengelig, eller for tilpassede tilberedninger på bestilling. Nivå av koding kan variere fra legemiddelnavn til strukturerte detaljer om den aktuelle legemiddelforpakningen som skal brukes.
For eksempel: 'Plaquenil'; 'Ibrutinib 420 mg tablett'; 'Rectinol salve, 50 g, tube'; or 'Hydrofiber bandasje med sølv'.

at0023.description: Den kliniske årsaken for bruken av legemiddelet.
at0023.comment: For eksempel: "Angina" eller "Migrene". Der det er mulig foretrekkes at den den kliniske indikasjonen blir kodet med en terminologi. Dette dataelementet tillater flere forekomster per legemiddel.

at0026.description: Dato/tidspunkt for når legemiddelet ble sist tatt av eller administrert til individet.
at0026.comment: For eksempel: siste gang warfarin ble tatt i forkant av innleggelse i sykehus, eller når siste dose ble administrert før overføring fra sykehus til sykehjem.

at0029.description: Ytterligere fritekstbeskrivelse av legemiddelerklæringen som ikke er registrert i andre felt.

at0030.comment: For eksempel "oral bruk", "intravenøst", "på huden" eller "enteralt". Det bør om mulig benyttes terminologi, f.eks. FEST, for å angi administreringsvei.

at0032.description: Fritekstbeskrivelse om bruken av det navngitte legemiddelet.

at0037.description: Den tiltenkte absolutte sluttdatoen for bruk av legemiddelet eller en tekstlig indikasjon på at legemiddelet vil bli brukt på ubestemt tid.
at0037.comment: Datatypen DV_DATE_TIME kan indikere en presis, absolutt dato og valgfri tid for den tiltenkte slutten på en begrenset legemiddelkur - for eksempel sluttpunktet for en kur med antibiotika som avsenderen nettopp har startet. Alternativet DV_CODED_TEXT kan også brukes for å registrere at legemiddelet er ment for ubestemt bruk, for eksempel potensielt livslang bruk av et antihypertensivt legemiddel.
